message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-8, latest commit). Your job: confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; the regression test actually DISCRIMINATES — fails if the fix is reverted). Be skeptical; try to find a hole.

FINDING K-OPS-1:
The lockfile-sync "reproducibility guard" re-resolved against live PyPI (no --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was failing on clean checkouts (main CI red). Fix: a single committed date file .uv-exclude-newer (2026-06-14T00:00:00Z) is read by BOTH ci.yml (3 compile commands) and scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh, passing --exclude-newer so resolution is pinned to a point-in-time index. The cutoff was chosen clear of a boundary release (arize-phoenix 17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic. All three locks regenerated. VERIFY reproducibility: run the regen and a fresh `uv pip compile ... --exclude-newer "$(cat .uv-exclude-newer | tr -d '[:space:]')"` and confirm the non-comment content matches the committed requirements.lock (uv 0.11.21 is available locally). Tests: test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned.

Read the actual changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path> — use "python -m pytest", not bare pytest, for your own runs; uv 0.11.21 is available for K-OPS-1). Judge ONLY K-OPS-1. Return CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name it); REJECTED if wrong or non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding K-OPS-1. Let me start by examining the changed files.

commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git show HEAD -- .github/workflows/ci.yml scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh .uv-exclude-newer | head -250
input.description: Show diff of CI and regen script

commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 9e1e25c..af0a260 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -103,7 +103,11 @@ jobs:
       # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
       # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
       - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
-        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
+        # --cov-fail-under ENFORCES a floor (#K-OPS-3): without it `pytest --cov` prints coverage
+        # but exits 0 at any level, so the coverage "gate" was vacuous — a refactor deleting tests
+        # or adding a large untested module would still pass green. The floor (90) sits below the
+        # current ~97% with headroom for environment-dependent skips; ratchet it up over time.
+        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90
 
   # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
   # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
@@ -129,21 +133,28 @@ jobs:
           # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
           # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
           python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
-          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
+          # Pin the RESOLUTION to a fixed point-in-time index (#K-OPS-1). Without --exclude-newer,
+          # uv resolves the newest in-range version of every (largely unconstrained, transitive)
+          # dep at CI wall-clock time, so this "reproducibility" gate goes red the instant ANY
+          # upstream publishes an in-range release — on PRs that touch no dependency. The date is
+          # the single source of truth in .uv-exclude-newer (regenerate_lockfiles.sh reads the same
+          # file), so the regen here matches the committed lock exactly. Bump that file to update.
+          EXCLUDE_NEWER="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .uv-exclude-newer)"
+          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
           # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
-            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
+            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
           # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
           # stay in sync too.
-          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
+          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
-            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
           # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
           # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
           printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
-          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
+          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
-            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
diff --git a/.uv-exclude-newer b/.uv-exclude-newer
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c6c3624
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.uv-exclude-newer
@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+2026-06-14T00:00:00Z
diff --git a/scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh b/scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
index d867651..34a468a 100755
--- a/scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
+++ b/scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
@@ -9,16 +9,34 @@
 #     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
 #
 # uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
+# The resolution is ALSO pinned to a fixed point-in-time index via --exclude-newer (#K-OPS-1): the
+# date lives in .uv-exclude-newer (single source of truth, read by CI too) so the regen is
+# deterministic. Without it, uv resolves the NEWEST in-range version of every (largely
+# unconstrained, transitive) dep at wall-clock time, so the committed lock would drift the instant
+# any upstream publishes an in-range release and the "in sync" gate would go red on unrelated PRs.
+# To intentionally pick up new releases, bump the date in .uv-exclude-newer and re-run this script.
+#
+# This regenerates ALL THREE gated lockfiles — requirements.lock, requirements-core.lock, AND
+# requirements-build.lock (#K-OPS-2): the build lock is gated by CI too, so omitting it left a
+# maintainer following this script still red on a build-backend bump with no scripted fix.
 set -euo pipefail
 
+cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
+
 UV_VERSION="0.11.21"
+EXCLUDE_NEWER="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .uv-exclude-newer)"
 
 if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
   echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
   python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
 fi
 
-uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock
-uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock
+uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes \
+  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements.lock
+uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes \
+  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-core.lock
+printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
+uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes \
+  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-build.lock
 
-echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock — review and commit them."
+echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock + requirements-build.lock (exclude-newer=$EXCLUDE_NEWER) — review and commit them."

content.text: The diff looks coherent. Now let me check the regression test and verify reproducibility by actually regenerating.

content.text: Now let me run the test and the actual reproducibility verification.

Now the critical reproducibility check: regenerate against the pinned date and diff against the committed lockfiles. Let me check the discrimination of the test too (does it fail if reverted?). The test only checks for `--exclude-newer` presence in CI text plus file existence/format — let me verify it actually discriminates and then do the real reproducibility regen.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pip install uv==0.11.21 -q 2>&1 | tail -2; uv --version

cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && EN="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .uv-exclude-newer)" && echo "cutoff=$EN" && \
uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EN" -o /tmp/req.regen.lock 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EN" -o /tmp/req-core.regen.lock 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in && \

content.text: Let me verify `_ci_text()` reads the actual ci.yml (so the test is bound to the real file), and note the discrimination scope.

1	"""Supply-chain / CI audit-gate regression guards (pass-2 #130, #131, #132, #145, #146, #147).
2	
3	These guard configuration that lives in .github/workflows/ci.yml and the committed lockfile.
4	The CI YAML cannot be unit-imported, so the guards are twofold:
5	  1. config-level: assert the hardened invocation is present in ci.yml (a revert to the buggy
6	     form fails the test);
7	  2. behavioral: run the exact allowlist-parsing bash loop against fixtures to prove it is
8	     newline-agnostic and comment/blank tolerant (the original `while read -r id` form drops a
9	     final line that lacks a trailing newline).
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import shutil
15	import subprocess
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
21	_CI = _ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"
22	_LOCK = _ROOT / "requirements.lock"
23	
24	# The hardened allowlist loop, lifted verbatim from ci.yml's audit job. Kept here so the
25	# behavioral test exercises the SAME logic the workflow runs.
26	_PARSE_LOOP = r"""
27	set -euo pipefail
28	IGNORES=()
29	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
30	  while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
31	    case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
32	    IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
33	  done < "$1"
34	fi
35	printf '%s\n' "${IGNORES[@]:-<none>}"
36	"""
37	
38	# The ORIGINAL buggy loop, for the revert-discrimination assertion in the behavioral test.
39	_PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY = r"""
40	IGNORES=""
41	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
42	  while read -r id; do [ -n "$id" ] && IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore-vuln $id"; done < "$1"
43	fi
44	printf '%s\n' "$IGNORES"
45	"""
46	
47	
48	def _ci_text() -> str:
49	    return _CI.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
50	
51	
52	def test_pip_audit_runs_strict_and_require_hashes():

content.text:
I have everything I need. Let me consolidate my assessment.

The fix is appropriate and complete:

**Root cause correct**: The original CI re-resolved against live PyPI with no point-in-time pin, so any in-range transitive publish would flip the diff gate red on unrelated PRs. Fixed at the right layer (the resolution invocation) via `--exclude-newer`.

**Single source of truth**: `.uv-exclude-newer` (2026-06-14T00:00:00Z) is read by both ci.yml (all 3 compile commands) and `regenerate_lockfiles.sh`, parsed identically (`tr -d '[:space:]'`), so CI and the script cannot drift.

**Reproducibility verified empirically**: I ran fresh `uv pip compile` for all three locks at the pinned cutoff with uv 0.11.21 — all three diff MATCH (non-comment content) against the committed locks. I also ran the actual `regenerate_lockfiles.sh` from an unrelated cwd; it correctly `cd`s to repo root and produces zero git drift on all three lockfiles.

**Tests pass and discriminate**: `test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned` and `test_regenerate_script_covers_all_gated_lockfiles` pass. I confirmed they discriminate: reverting `--exclude-newer` from ci.yml or removing the date file fails the first; removing the build lock or `--exclude-newer` from the script fails the second.

One residual gap worth naming (not blocking): the regression tests are purely config-level string-presence assertions. They prove the flag/file/build-lock are *present* but do NOT assert reproducibility itself (that the committed locks actually re-resolve to the same content at the pinned date). A future dependency bump without regenerating the locks would slip past these tests and only be caught by the CI diff gate (which is intact). This matches the file's own stated "config-level" guard philosophy and is acceptable, but it is a real limitation of test discrimination scope.

input.rationale: Root cause is correctly identified and fixed at the right layer: the lockfile-sync gate re-resolved against live PyPI with no point-in-time pin, so any in-range transitive publish flipped the diff gate red on clean checkouts. Fix adds --exclude-newer to all three CI compile commands AND to regenerate_lockfiles.sh, sourcing the cutoff from a single committed file (.uv-exclude-newer, 2026-06-14T00:00:00Z) parsed identically in both places — eliminating CI/script drift. Empirically verified reproducibility with uv 0.11.21: fresh `uv pip compile --exclude-newer` for requirements.lock, requirements-core.lock, and requirements-build.lock all diff MATCH (non-comment content) against the committed locks; running regenerate_lockfiles.sh from an unrelated cwd produces zero git drift on all three (the script correctly cds to repo root). The cutoff (2026-06-14) sits clear of the arize-phoenix 17.6/17.7 boundary. Regression tests test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned and test_regenerate_script_covers_all_gated_lockfiles pass and discriminate: removing --exclude-newer from ci.yml or deleting the date file fails the first; dropping the build lock or the flag from the script fails the second.
concerns: Non-blocking, named: the regression tests are config-level string-presence assertions (flag present, date file exists/well-formed, build lock referenced). They do NOT assert reproducibility itself — i.e. that the committed locks actually re-resolve to identical content at the pinned date. A dependency bump committed without regenerating the locks would pass these unit tests and be caught only by the live CI diff gate (which remains intact). Consistent with the file's stated config-level guard philosophy and acceptable, but a real limit on test discrimination scope.

- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py (test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned, test_regenerate_script_covers_all_gated_lockfiles)

The fix is appropriate and complete. I empirically reproduced all three lockfiles at the pinned cutoff with uv 0.11.21 (exact non-comment match), confirmed the regen script is path-robust and drift-free, and verified the regression tests discriminate on revert. One non-blocking limitation noted: the tests guard config presence, not reproducibility itself — that remains the job of the live CI diff gate.
